headline: Founder, President and CEO, Rewiring America. Building the lower cost, bountiful future for everyone.
current_position_description: Built and led affordable housing coalition and campaign with over 120-member organizations from business, nonprofit, faith-based and philanthropic communities to address state affordable housing crisis. Led first statewide affordable housing bond campaign in 2006 to secure funds for construction of affordable homeownership and rental units for working families, winning 66% of vote and ultimately leveraging +$1 billion in affordable housing development that created thousands of jobs. Secured systemic regulatory and legislative reform in support of long-term affordable housing development and homelessness reduction initiatives.
latest_school_location: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
latest_grade: Cum Laude, Public Interest Law Scholar
investor_title: Ari Matusiak is based out of United States and is the Founder and Co-Chair of Power Forward Communities. Ari previously worked at Young Invincibles as a Founder and Chair. Ari Matusiak attended Georgetown University Law Center.

positionList.title: Founder and Director
positionList.description: Built and led affordable housing coalition and campaign with over 120-member organizations from business, nonprofit, faith-based and philanthropic communities to address state affordable housing crisis. Led first statewide affordable housing bond campaign in 2006 to secure funds for construction of affordable homeownership and rental units for working families, winning 66% of vote and ultimately leveraging +$1 billion in affordable housing development that created thousands of jobs. Secured systemic regulatory and legislative reform in support of long-term affordable housing development and homelessness reduction initiatives.

positionList.title: Special Assistant to the President & Director of Private Sector Engagement
positionList.description: Built and led the team responsible for engaging the private sector in the development and implementation of Administration jobs and competitiveness policy and domestic and international public-private partnerships. Managed Administration’s relationship with Fortune 500 CEOs, Wall Street firms, venture-backed enterprises and industry groups. Oversaw and managed the engagement of over 200 senior Administration officials – including Cabinet members and senior White House officials – with the private sector. Worked across and developed expertise in multiple policy areas, including jobs and competitiveness, budget, tax, fiscal and immigration policy, energy and climate, international trade and investment, healthcare, mobility and cybersecurity and national security matters. TS / SCI clearance.

positionList.title: Founder and Co-Chair
positionList.location: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
positionList.description: Power Forward Communities is a coalition of some of the country’s most trusted housing, community investment and climate organizations dedicated to lowering costs for American households and increasing affordable housing across the country. We are saving homeowners and renters money, reinvesting in communities and reducing emissions along the way. Power Forward Communities was awarded a $2 billion, seven-year National Clean Investment Fund (NCIF) grant from the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to provide capital for these solutions, with a specific focus on low and moderate income households.

positionList.title: Founder and Chair
positionList.description: Co-founded and -led development of Young Invincibles into leading national organization focused on creating economic opportunity for 18- to 34-year-olds. Led national coalition of youth-focused organizations that successfully advocated as part of Affordable Care Act enactment for ability of young adults to remain on parents’ health insurance until age 26. Successfully negotiated with senior White House leadership and higher education lobby to include college health plans under Affordable Care Act, providing ~3 million young adults and families per year with quality health insurance.

positionList.title: Vice President of Strategy and Community Impact
positionList.description: Senior executive at one of oldest and largest community foundations in country with ~$1 billion in assets. Stewarded institutional priority- and strategy-setting with Board of Directors and CEO. Directed ~$10M in annual strategic investments, institutional, regional and national partnerships and public policy initiatives. Restructured Foundation grantmaking, aligning investments to areas of strategic focus and tied to impact metrics. Developed first statewide “Initiative for Nonprofit Excellence,” investing in management performance of state nonprofits. Led state and national funding partnership resulting in changing state constitution to re-enfranchise ex-offenders. Cultivated high-level donors and prospects and represented Foundation in state agency and national advisory boards.

positionList.title: Chief Strategy Officer
positionList.description: Chief Strategy Officer, the head of market development and external affairs at Renovate America, the largest residential energy efficiency and renewable energy financing platform in the country at the time.

positionList.title: Founder, President and CEO
positionList.location: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
positionList.description: Rewiring America is focused on delivering value for American households and communities. With better, more efficient, electric machines, we can take five kitchen table decisions — how we heat our air and water, cook our food, dry our clothes, what kind of cars we drive and where the power comes from for those things — and turn them into average annual savings of $3,000 for families. Beyond these direct benefits, there are also shared benefits. Millions of new, local jobs that cannot be automated or offshored. $300 million in additional dollars flowing through the average sized community. Every year. There are 1 billion machines across our 129 million households. Electrifying them will cut the amount of energy we use in the economy in half. And it will reduce our energy-related emissions by 42 percent. This is the freedom to live better. It is a future we can all share and have. It is the one we are working to achieve.
